title: Which segment should they enter first?
guidance: Reward clear prioritisation with a defensible reason. Penalise hedging that recommends multiple segments or conditions the answer on more research.

runner_input.instruction: Based on the market data below, recommend which segment this company should enter first as its beachhead. State your recommendation and the rationale.
runner_input.constraints: Pick one segment. Give a clear rationale in 2–3 sentences. Do not hedge with 'it depends' or recommend a phased approach.

source_blocks.text: SynthSoft is a UK regulatory-reporting software startup. It has £1.2m seed funding and a 6-person team. It has built an MVP for automated ESG data collection from supplier questionnaires. It is considering three segment options for its first go-to-market motion.

items: Segment A — Enterprise (1,000+ employees): high ACV (£80–120k), long sales cycles (9–14 months), requires SOC2 and enterprise integrations not yet built
Segment B — Mid-market (100–500 employees): ACV £15–25k, 3–5 month sales cycles, ESG mandate urgency highest here, buyer is CFO or sustainability lead with budget
Segment C — SME (<100 employees): ACV £3–6k, fast sales cycles, low switching cost, but ESG mandate exempts firms under 250 employees through 2027

rubric_notes: Mid-market (Segment B) is correct: regulatory urgency is highest there, ACV is large enough to matter, cycles are manageable for a seed-stage team.
Enterprise (A) is premature — missing SOC2 and integrations are blockers, not considerations.
SME (C) is disqualified by the regulatory exemption — no urgency until 2027.
Penalise any response that does not pick a single segment or treats the exemption as minor.
